Description: Reduced left prefrontal cortical activity relative to right, associated with depressive symptoms and reduced approach motivation. Often identified on qEEG as frontal alpha asymmetry.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: qEEG; frontal alpha asymmetry assessment; PHQ-9

Description: Persistent repetitive negative thinking; self-focused attention; difficulty disengaging from negative content. May involve overactive default mode network.
Possible_Target_Regions: Left DLPFC; default mode network modulation
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: PHQ-9; RRS (Ruminative Response Scale); cognitive assessment

Description: Marked loss of interest and pleasure; reduced reward sensitivity; motivational deficits. Associated with ventral striatal hypoactivity.
Possible_Target_Regions: Left DLPFC; ventral striatal circuits (indirectly via PFC)
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: SHAPS (Snaith-Hamilton Pleasure Scale); MADRS anhedonia items; PHQ-9 items 1-2

Description: Elevated physiological arousal; exaggerated startle response; hypervigilance to threat; sleep disruption; muscle tension. Often involves right PFC/amygdala overactivity.
Candidate_Modalities: rTMS (inhibitory right DLPFC); CES; HRV biofeedback; Neurofeedback
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: GAD-7; PCL-5 hyperarousal cluster; HRV metrics; skin conductance

Description: Impairment in planning, working memory, cognitive flexibility, inhibitory control, and goal-directed behavior. Common across multiple neurological and psychiatric conditions.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: BRIEF-A/2; Trail Making Test B; Stroop; Digit Span; Wisconsin Card Sorting

Description: Difficulty initiating sleep; prolonged sleep latency (>30 min); pre-sleep cognitive arousal; hyperarousal at bedtime.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: ISI; sleep diary (SOL metric); PSQI; GAD-7

Description: Frequent nighttime awakenings; difficulty returning to sleep; reduced sleep efficiency; fragmented sleep architecture.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: ISI; sleep diary (WASO, SE% metrics); PSQI; actigraphy if available

Description: Abnormal heart rate variability; excessive sympathetic tone; reduced parasympathetic function; somatic symptoms of anxiety.
Associated_Conditions: PTSD; GAD; Fibromyalgia; Chronic fatigue; Autonomic neuropathy
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: HRV assessment; blood pressure variability; tilt table if indicated; GAD-7

Description: Predominantly inattentive symptoms: difficulty sustaining attention, poor organization, forgetfulness, distractibility without significant hyperactivity.
Candidate_Modalities: Neurofeedback (theta/beta — NOT supported by blinded outcomes); tDCS (emerging); TMS (emerging)
Evidence_Level: Emerging — NOT supported by blinded outcomes for NF
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: ASRS inattention subscale; Conners inattention scale; BRIEF; CPT

Description: Predominantly hyperactive-impulsive symptoms: restlessness, fidgeting, difficulty waiting, impulsive decisions, interrupting.
Possible_Target_Regions: Right inferior frontal gyrus; SMA; basal ganglia circuits
Candidate_Modalities: Neurofeedback (SMR — NOT supported by blinded outcomes); tDCS (emerging)
Evidence_Level: Emerging — NOT supported by blinded outcomes for NF
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: ASRS hyperactivity subscale; Conners impulsivity scale; BRIEF inhibition scale

Description: Amplified pain processing in CNS; widespread hyperalgesia; allodynia; pain not fully explained by peripheral pathology.
Associated_Conditions: Fibromyalgia; Chronic pain; IBS; TMJ; Chronic migraine
Possible_Target_Regions: Primary motor cortex (M1); DLPFC; thalamic circuits
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: BPI; VAS/NRS; Central Sensitization Inventory; pain catastrophizing scale

Description: Burning, shooting, or electrical pain quality; numbness or tingling; evidence of nerve damage or dysfunction.
Associated_Conditions: Diabetic neuropathy; Post-stroke pain; SCI pain; CRPS
Possible_Target_Regions: M1 contralateral to pain; thalamic pain circuits
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: SF-MPQ-2 neuropathic subscale; DN4; NRS; pain quality descriptors

Description: Reversible visual, sensory, or speech symptoms preceding or accompanying migraine headache. Related to cortical spreading depolarization.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Headache diary; aura tracking; MIDAS; HIT-6

Description: Weakness, spasticity, or loss of fine motor control in upper extremity following stroke. Related to disrupted motor cortex and corticospinal tract.
Possible_Target_Regions: Ipsilesional M1 (excitatory); contralesional M1 (inhibitory); bilateral protocols
Candidate_Modalities: rTMS (literature-supported); tDCS (emerging); combined with physical therapy
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: FMA-UE; ARAT; grip strength; finger dexterity; modified Rankin Scale

Description: Persistent attention, memory, and processing speed deficits following traumatic brain injury. May include executive dysfunction and emotional dysregulation.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: MoCA; Trail Making Test; BRIEF-A; neurocognitive battery; PCL-5 if trauma-related

Description: Intrusive, unwanted, repetitive thoughts that cause significant distress. May involve contamination fears, harm thoughts, symmetry/ordering concerns, or religious/sexual obsessions.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Y-BOCS obsession subscale; OCI-R; CGI

Description: Repetitive behaviors performed to reduce anxiety from obsessions: washing, checking, counting, ordering. Also includes behavioral compulsions without clear obsessive trigger.
Candidate_Modalities: Deep TMS (BrainsWay H7); rTMS (SMA); DBS (HDE for severe OCD)
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Y-BOCS compulsion subscale; behavioral tracking; functional impairment assessment

Description: PTSD-specific hyperarousal cluster: exaggerated startle, hypervigilance, sleep disturbance, irritability, concentration difficulty following traumatic exposure.
Associated_Conditions: PTSD; Complex PTSD; Acute stress disorder
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: PCL-5 hyperarousal cluster (items 16-20); CAPS-5; HRV metrics

Description: Depersonalization, derealization, emotional numbing, identity disruption. May occur in PTSD and other trauma-related conditions.
Associated_Conditions: PTSD (dissociative subtype); Complex PTSD; DID; BPD
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: DES-II (Dissociative Experiences Scale); MDI; CAPS-5 dissociative items

Description: Resting tremor at 4-6 Hz, typically asymmetric, pill-rolling character. Hallmark of Parkinson's disease motor presentation.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: UPDRS tremor items; accelerometry; clinical tremor rating

Description: Action tremor (postural and kinetic) at 4-12 Hz, predominantly upper extremity, bilateral. Worsens with intentional movement.
Candidate_Modalities: DBS (VIM — FDA approved); MRgFUS thalamotomy (FDA cleared)
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Clinical tremor rating; functional disability assessment; Archimedes spiral

Description: Abnormal excessive or synchronous neuronal activity causing seizures. Focal or generalized. Drug-refractory pattern.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: EEG; seizure diary; video-EEG monitoring; MRI

Description: Co-occurring anxiety and depressive symptoms that do not clearly meet criteria for either disorder alone, or comorbid MDD+GAD. Common in clinical practice.
Associated_Conditions: MDD with comorbid anxiety; Mixed anxiety-depression
Possible_Target_Regions: Left DLPFC (bilateral protocols may be superior)
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: PHQ-9; GAD-7; PHQ-4 combined screener

Description: Persistent symptom burden despite adequate trials of ≥2 evidence-based treatments. Suggests need for alternative modalities or augmentation strategies.
Associated_Conditions: TRD; Treatment-resistant anxiety; Refractory OCD; Drug-resistant epilepsy
Candidate_Modalities: Depends on condition — escalation to neuromodulation
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Treatment history documentation; adequate trial verification; pharmacogenomic testing if available

Description: Intense desire for substance or behavior; loss of control; continued use despite consequences; withdrawal symptoms.
Associated_Conditions: Smoking cessation; Opioid withdrawal; Alcohol use disorder
Possible_Target_Regions: Bilateral insula/PFC (smoking); auricular vagus nerve (opioid withdrawal)
Candidate_Modalities: Deep TMS (H4-coil for smoking); taVNS (Sparrow for opioid withdrawal)
Evidence_Level: Guideline-supported (Deep TMS smoking); Literature-supported (taVNS opioid withdrawal)
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Fagerström (smoking); COWS (opioid); craving VAS; substance use history

Description: Electroencephalographic pattern showing greater alpha power (indicating lower activity) over left frontal regions relative to right. Associated with depression and withdrawal motivation.
Candidate_Modalities: rTMS (excitatory left DLPFC); Neurofeedback (alpha asymmetry training)
Evidence_Level: Literature-supported (as biomarker; emerging as treatment target)
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: qEEG recording; alpha power asymmetry calculation (F3-F4)

Description: Elevated theta-to-beta power ratio on qEEG, historically associated with ADHD. Recent research questions its diagnostic specificity and treatment-predictive value.
Candidate_Modalities: Neurofeedback (theta/beta ratio training — NOT supported by blinded outcomes for ADHD)
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: qEEG recording; theta/beta ratio calculation; normative database comparison

Description: Impairment of language production and/or comprehension following stroke affecting language-dominant hemisphere. Broca's (expressive), Wernicke's (receptive), or global.
Candidate_Modalities: rTMS (emerging); tDCS (emerging) — combined with speech therapy
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Boston Naming Test; Token Test; WAB (Western Aphasia Battery); discourse analysis

Description: Persistent, disabling fatigue disproportionate to activity level. May involve central fatigue mechanisms. Common comorbidity across multiple conditions.
Associated_Conditions: Fibromyalgia; Depression; TBI; MS; Chronic fatigue syndrome
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: Fatigue Severity Scale; Multidimensional Fatigue Inventory; ISI (sleep-related fatigue)

Description: Difficulty modulating emotional responses; frequent intense emotional reactions; poor frustration tolerance; rapid mood shifts.
Assessment_Inputs_Needed: DERS (Difficulties in Emotion Regulation Scale); BRIEF emotional control subscale; mood diary
